headline: The agentic tooling stack that defined the quarter, orchestration, agent roles, and context management, cooled while cost and quality concerns moved to the front.
body: The techniques that led prior windows, orchestration, agent roles, context management, and skills, all weakened this quarter, and AI agents, recursive self-improvement, Language Server Protocol, and Model Context Protocol slipped into lagging momentum. Replacing them as new entrants are cost analysis, quality control, automated testing, and LLM, signaling a shift from building agent scaffolding toward measuring and validating it. The economics of that shift surface directly: bootstrapped founders are reassessing the ROI of AI coding tools, while one engineer reports LLM-driven code generation eroding their career prospects. Separately, OpenAI is reframing its interface strategy, with a senior employee declaring chat dead as work continues on a super app.

cited_items.title: OpenAI is still working on that ‘super app’
The ROI of AI coding looks different when you are a bootstrapped founder
LLMs are eroding my software engineering career and I don't know what to do
